2603.17548.title: CLeAN: Continual Learning Adaptive Normalization in Dynamic Environments
2603.17548.paper_summary: Artificial intelligence systems predominantly rely on static data distributions, making them ineffective in dynamic real-world environments, such as cybersecurity, autonomous transportation, or finance, where data shifts frequently. Continual learning offers a potential solution by enabling models to learn from sequential data while retaining prior knowledge. However, a critical and underexplored issue in this domain is data normalization. Conventional normalization methods, such as min-max scaling, presuppose access to the entire dataset, which is incongruent with the sequential nature of continual learning. In this paper we introduce Continual Learning Adaptive Normalization (CLeAN), a novel adaptive normalization technique designed for continual learning in tabular data. CLeAN involves the estimation of global feature scales using learnable parameters that are updated via an Exponential Moving Average (EMA) module, enabling the model to adapt to evolving data distributions. Through comprehensive evaluations on two datasets and various continual learning strategies, including Resevoir Experience Replay, A-GEM, and EwC we demonstrate that CLeAN not only improves model performance on new data but also mitigates catastrophic forgetting. The findings underscore the importance of adaptive normalization in enhancing the stability and effectiveness of tabular data, offering a novel perspective on the use of normalization to preserve knowledge in dynamic learning environments.
2603.17548.paper_summary_zh: 人工智慧系統主要依賴靜態數據分佈，使其在動態的現實環境中（例如網絡安全、自主運輸或金融）無法有效運作，因為這些環境中的數據經常變動。持續學習提供了一個潛在的解決方案，通過使模型能夠從序列數據中學習，同時保留先前的知識。然而，在這個領域中，一個關鍵且未被充分探討的問題是數據正規化。傳統的正規化方法，如最小-最大縮放，假設可以訪問整個數據集，這與持續學習的序列特性不相符。在本文中，我們介紹了持續學習自適應正規化（CLeAN），這是一種專為表格數據中的持續學習設計的新型自適應正規化技術。CLeAN 涉及使用可學習參數來估計全局特徵尺度，這些參數通過指數移動平均（EMA）模塊進行更新，使模型能夠適應不斷變化的數據分佈。通過對兩個數據集和各種持續學習策略（包括儲存經驗重播、A-GEM 和 EwC）進行全面評估，我們展示了 CLeAN 不僅提高了模型在新數據上的性能，還減輕了災難性遺忘。研究結果強調了自適應正規化在增強表格數據的穩定性和有效性方面的重要性，並為在動態學習環境中使用正規化以保留知識提供了一種新視角。
2603.17548.author: Isabella Marasco et.al.
2603.17548.authors: Isabella Marasco, Davide Evangelista, Elena Loli Piccolomini, Michele Colajanni
